heading: Special use permits
label: 1441 Special use permits

children.text: to establish conditions of access to and use of any sanctuary resource; or
to promote public use and understanding of a sanctuary resource.

children.heading: Public notice required
children.label: b Public notice required
children.text: The Secretary shall provide appropriate public notice before identifying any category of activity subject to a special use permit under subsection (a).
shall authorize the conduct of an activity only if that activity is compatible with the purposes for which the sanctuary is designated and with protection of sanctuary resources;
shall not authorize the conduct of any activity for a period of more than 5 years unless renewed by the Secretary;
shall require that activities carried out under the permit be conducted in a manner that does not destroy, cause the loss of, or injure sanctuary resources; and

shall require the permittee to purchase and maintain comprehensive general liability insurance, or post an equivalent bond, against claims arising out of activities conducted under the permit and to agree to hold the United States harmless against such claims.

children.heading: Assessment and collection
children.label: 1 Assessment and collection
children.text: The Secretary may assess and collect fees for the conduct of any activity under a permit issued under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: costs incurred, or expected to be incurred, by the Secretary in issuing the permit;

children.label: B
children.text: costs incurred, or expected to be incurred, by the Secretary as a direct result of the conduct of the activity for which the permit is issued, including costs of monitoring the conduct of the activity; and

children.label: C
children.text: an amount which represents the fair market value of the use of the sanctuary resource.

children.label: A
children.text: for issuing and administering permits under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: for expenses of managing national marine sanctuaries.

children.heading: Waiver or reduction of fees
children.label: 4 Waiver or reduction of fees
children.text: The Secretary may accept in-kind contributions in lieu of a fee under paragraph (2)(C), or waive or reduce any fee assessed under this subsection for any activity that does not derive profit from the access to or use of sanctuary resources.
suspend or revoke the permit without compensation to the permittee and without liability to the United States;
assess a civil penalty in accordance with ; orsection 1437 of this title

children.heading: Reports
children.label: f Reports
children.text: Each person issued a permit under this section shall submit an annual report to the Secretary not later than December 31 of each year which describes activities conducted under that permit and revenues derived from such activities during the year.

children.heading: Fishing
children.label: g Fishing
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be considered to require a person to obtain a permit under this section for the conduct of any fishing activities in a national marine sanctuary.
